briefdescription.para: This function detects the PHY address by looking for successful MII status register contents (PHY register 1).

detaileddescription.para: It looks for a PHY that supports auto-negotiation and 10Mbps full-duplex and half-duplex. So, this code won't work for PHYs that don't support those features, but it's a bit more general purpose than matching a specific PHY manufacturer ID.

Note also that on some (older) Xilinx ML4xx boards, PHY address 0 does not properly respond to this query. But, since the default is 0 and assuming no other address responds, then it seems to work OK.

parameterdescription.para: Axi Ethernet driver instance

simplesect.para: The address of the PHY (defaults to 0 if none detected)

detaileddescription.para: This works with the marvell PHY common on ML40x evaluation boards

parameterdescription.para: is the loopback speed 10, 100, or 1000 Mbit

briefdescription.para: Set the MAC addresses in the frame.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the frame.
is the Destination MAC address.

briefdescription.para: Set the frame type for the specified frame.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the frame.
is the Type to set in frame.

briefdescription.para: This function places a pattern in the payload section of a frame.

detaileddescription.para: The pattern is a 8 bit incrementing series of numbers starting with 0. Once the pattern reaches 256, then the pattern changes to a 16 bit incrementing pattern: 0, 1, 2, ... 254, 255, 00, 00, 00, 01, 00, 02, ...

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the frame to change.
is the number of bytes in the payload that will be set.

briefdescription.para: Set the frame VLAN info for the specified frame.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the frame.
is the VlanValue insertion position to set in frame.
is the 4 bytes Vlan value (TPID, Priority, CFI, VID) to be set in frame.

briefdescription.para: Set the frame type for the specified frame.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the frame.
is the Type to set in frame.
is the VLAN friendly adjusted insertion position to set in frame.

briefdescription.para: This function places a pattern in the payload section of a frame.

detaileddescription.para: The pattern is a 8 bit incrementing series of numbers starting with 0. Once the pattern reaches 256, then the pattern changes to a 16 bit incrementing pattern: 0, 1, 2, ... 254, 255, 00, 00, 00, 01, 00, 02, ...

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the frame to change.
is the number of bytes in the payload that will be set.
is the VLAN friendly adjusted insertion position to set in frame.

briefdescription.para: This function verifies the frame data against a CheckFrame.

detaileddescription.para: Validation occurs by comparing the ActualFrame to the header of the CheckFrame. If the headers match, then the payload of ActualFrame is verified for the same pattern Util_FrameSetPayloadData() generates.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to a frame containing the 14 byte header that should be present in the ActualFrame parameter.
is a pointer to a frame to validate.

briefdescription.para: This function sets all bytes of a frame to 0.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the frame itself.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the PHY to loopback mode.

detaileddescription.para: This works with the marvell PHY common on ML40x evaluation boards.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance of the AxiEthernet component.
is the loopback speed 10, 100, or 1000 Mbit.

briefdescription.para: This function is called by example code when an error is detected.

detaileddescription.para: It can be set as a breakpoint with a debugger or it can be used to print out the given message if there is a UART or STDIO device.

parameterdescription.para: is the text explaining the error

briefdescription.para: For Microblaze we use an assembly loop that is roughly the same regardless of optimization level, although caches and memory access time can make the delay vary.

detaileddescription.para: Just keep in mind that after resetting or updating the PHY modes, the PHY typically needs time to recover.

briefdescription.para: This function configures the internal phy for SGMII and 1000baseX modes.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance of the AxiEthernet component.
is the loopback speed 10, 100, or 1000 Mbit.

briefdescription.para: This file implements the utility functions for the Axi Ethernet example code.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a asa 6/30/10 First release based on the ll temac driver 3.01a srt 02/03/13 Added support for SGMII mode (CR 676793). 02/14/13 Added support for Zynq (CR 681136). 3.02a srt 04/24/13 Modified parameter *_SGMII_PHYADDR to *_PHYADDR, the config parameter C_PHYADDR applies to SGMII/1000BaseX modes of operation and added support for 1000BaseX mode (CR 704195). Added function *_ConfigureInternalPhy() for this purpose. 04/24/13 Added support for RGMII mode. 3.02a srt 08/06/13 Fixed CR 717949: Configures external Marvel 88E1111 PHY based on the axi ethernet physical interface type and allows to operate in specific interface mode without changing jumpers on the Microblaze board. 5.4 adk 07/12/16 Added Support for TI PHY DP83867. ms 04/05/17 Added tabspace for return statements in functions for proper documentation while generating doxygen. 5.17 ml 11/15/23 Fix compilation errors reported with -std=c2x compiler flag
